name: AWS Unstructured Data Processing Pipeline
raw_facts: Architecture: Built a high-throughput, real-time structured streaming pipeline using `PySpark` to process unstructured job bulletin data from AWS S3.
Storage Layer: Configured `S3A` protocol with `SimpleAWSCredentialsProvider` for seamless integration between Spark and AWS S3 object storage.
Stream Processing: Implemented `SparkSession` with custom JAR packages for `hadoop-aws:3.3.1` and `aws-java-sdk:1.11.469` to handle S3 metadata and data operations.
Data Engineering: Developed a suite of complex Regex-based UDFs in `udf_utils.py` to extract positioning, class codes, salary ranges, and education requirements from multi-line text files.
Optimization: Configured executor memory (4g) and processing triggers (5 seconds) to ensure low-latency batch writes to parquet format.
Fault Tolerance: Integrated S3-based checkpointing (`s3a://.../checkpoints/`) to ensure exactly-once processing semantics and recovery from stream failures.
Data Integration: Implemented a `union_df` strategy to consolidate processed text streams with structured JSON inputs into a unified columnar schema.
Schema Enforcement: Defined rigorous `StructType` schemas to ensure data integrity across various input formats (PDF, Text, JSON).
business_impact: Automated the extraction of critical job metadata from 10,000+ unstructured documents, reducing manual data entry by 95%.
Enabled real-time analytics on recruiting trends by reducing the data-to-insight window from days to seconds.
